name: Botzum, Lida E.
description: She's brisk and energetic, And she's decided, quite. 'Tis said, that in this city, She never spent a night. Tho' little, she is mighty, As you will all agree, She surely is no lesser light In English 13 B. Our class president; one of the 'little things that count'; expounds the joys of Algebra to the Ninth Grade.

name: Caldwell, Laura Ethel
description: Quiet, calm, observing, Always very kind, Studies without ceasing, Known to be a grind, Dotes upon Muskingum, Thinks the men there are fine— But when she gets to teaching She'll keep the 'kids' in line. Another Climatology 'Stude' devours books whole; is somewhat interested in tree-skinning.

name: Curtice, Nelia
major: Bachelor of Arts, 1914, University of Akron
clubs: President of the Y. W. C. A.
description: A live wire in Dramatic Club. Shines in History of Ed, they say; Always a little late to class But gets there just the same, you see, Optimistic and energetic, But flirts occasionally. refuses to sit in the front row in Mr. Layton's class; chief instigator of the Y. W. C. A.; if you want anything started, come to Nelia.

name: Cushman, Ella M.
major: Oberlin College—two years, Akron City Normal School—two years
description: Although she's small of stature, She's very far from frail; Her face is always smiling, As a friend, she'll never fail. She's our honored secretary, And one of the D. S. girls, And while she's often merry, She keeps out of social whirls. comes to us from Oberlin.

name: Kellmer, Earl B.
major: Bachelor of Arts, Adelbert College, Western Reserve University
description: Serious but often clever; He is energetic ever. Believes in Woman Suffrage. So got married in good season; Studies Climate under Olson Just to exercise his reason. The only man in the class—and he's married!!

name: Theiss, Lily M.
major: Bachelor of Arts, University of Akron
description: She's busy every minute With some project quite worth while: She always "gets there with the goods" And wears a welcome smile. With her favorite "English Journal" She spends many a happy (?) hour: And she champions the Kaiser With a deal of warmth and power. Optimistic—usually; has considerable common sense; if you need any help in starting something, call on her.

name: Townsend, Helen L.
clubs: Secretary of the Dramatic Club for a while
description: Tall and dark, with regal mien, With a keen and searching glance, She knows the laws of hygiene For every circumstance. Dramatic, positive indeed, She views you from afar; Her favorite mode of traveling Is the Kent and Ravenna car. plays the role of "Mutt" to a certain little "Jeff" of the Junior Class.

name: Landis, Cloyce Ivan
description: Slow but accurate, and with that dogged determination to stick to a thing until it is finished, which certainly will aid him after graduation. Has also been the "legal adviser" of the executive committee of the Athletic Association. Surely Kent State Normal may some day boast of having such a one on her list of graduates.

name: Hotchkiss, Hilda
motto: "Still waters run deep."
description: Hilda is quiet, studious and as unassuming and sweet as a wild rose. Always ready to render help where it is needed but never pushes herself forward.

name: Renneckar, Gertrude Idella
high school: Baltic High School, Canton Summer Normal
motto: "Like winds in summer sighing, Her voice is low and sweet."
description: Although Miss Renneckar has been with us but a short time, she has already made a place for herself at Kent State Normal, because of her friendly ways and pleasant smile. Is a diligent student very much interested in her work.

name: Ely, Edna Erle
description: A girl with perfect sincerity and love for all that is genuine. She has a big heart and a good handshake for everybody. She is blessed with plain reason and with common-sense.

name: Hazel, Lucille
description: Lucille has a winning way and a pleasing smile. She is the jolliest of girls, never too busy to do a favor and a sure cure for the blues.

name: Olin, Leona Genevieve
motto: "A blush is no language only a dubious flag signal, which may mean either of two contradictories."
description: Leona blushes at even the merest "rustle." However, all of her time is not spent in listening to a rustle. She plays a good game of tennis and likes to scout around the country.

name: Chamberlain, Ruth
motto: "A loyal heart, a spirit brave. A soul that is pure and true."
description: Her nose constantly points heavenward. Just a minute; is that Ruth or Charlotte? To be sure, it is Ruth with her blazing crown of glory.

name: Thomas, Crilla Belle
description: Here is a good example of the typical American girl. She is conscientious and sincere in her work as a student, but not what you would exactly call a saint, either. Crilla is game for everything going. When midnight promenades and such stunts as hypnosis need some daring spirit to put them through, Crilla is always "Johnny on the spot." Is also a great basketball enthusiast.

name: Bissell, Ora Isabelle
motto: "If at first you don't succeed, Try, and try some more."
description: Ora is noted for her patience, good humor and ready smile. Cat naps are her great delight. Sometimes gets a little bit (?) excited, and then the red roses in her cheeks blush redder still, but "blushing is the color of virtue." "My Stars!" We can hear that giggle yet.

name: Miller, Bertha Caroline
motto:
"Here's to a girlie who's ever true.
Most always is wearing some shade of blue.
When one first sees her, she looks so shy,
But she goes to class and makes the words fly.
Her eyes are so bright and her cheeks so rosy,
She straightway reminds one of a dainty posy.
Of Kent State Normal she is a pillar,
This girlie's name is Bertha Miller."

name: Bay, Frederick Dewitt
high school: Rich Hill High School, Muskingum College, Wooster Academy and University
motto: "With that characteristic spirit 'you will have to show me,' stamped on his features, Bay has made a success on all sides in school activities. Is a very ardent reader of 'The Pathfinder' and 'Nautilus,' as has been proven by his reports in class."

name: Wait, Corinne
motto:
"This studious little lassie
Is busy all the time;
One so quiet and so faithful
Is very hard to find."

name: Catherine, Richards
description: Catherine is learning to cook. Her favorite amusement is whispering in History of Ed. class. "She hides herself within herself," not even her classmates really know her. Boys are the least of her worries.

name: Ruby Clarissa, Hahn
motto: "If things don't go to suit you, just tell them that they have to."
description: Studies do not worry her at all. A girl loyal to every obligation and responsibility.

name: Margaret E., Barr
description: Our little girl with large bright eyes, How many hearts she's tantalized! She's jolly, witty, sweet, Her favorite vegetable is beet. Our unknown novelist, who, although she may gain great honors in this field, will some day give up all this fame, and turn her interest to the Pennsylvania Senate, thus utilizing her Domestic Science training at K. S. N.

name: Ellen, Peterson
motto: "Look forward not backward, And lend a hand."
description: "Pete" is genuine, earnest and faithful in the smallest details of her work. She is clever for she can hit the nail on the head every time. Witty and jolly, she is one who would make much brighter any sort of place.

name: Ruth, Priday
description: There is a young lady who goes to school And strictly obeys most every rule; She is a darling little creature, And says she came to be a teacher. Tho' I'd be willing to bet a dollar That she'd have just one lovely scholar.

name: Lena, Macdonald
description: If you want something done here is the girl who will do it. If you want something said, here is the girl who will say it. When she puts her shoulder to the wheel, things move or "bust." Lena sure can keep a secret too. How many Normalites knew about that lucky mining engineer now in Case?

name: Macintosh, Sara
description: Sara's cheerful disposition has made many a pessimist change his point of view. We hate to say that Sara is untruthful, but we have often heard her say that History of Education is her favorite subject.

name: Mikesell, Ray Everett
description: "Mike" hails from near that place known as Newark. He is another one of those pilgrims who came to Kent to obtain a professional education. "Mike's" chief assets are an optimistic point of view and an endless stock of patience. His fondest recollections of K. S. N. are the happy hours spent in the Department of Education.

name: Warring, Ethel Bernice
description: A joker as we all know. Whether she will consider her kindergarten children a joke or not, is yet to be seen. Yes, she does take things seriously sometimes (?) and then oh my! she is serious. Is very original in everything she does and says.

name: Hanlon, Marjorie Gladys
description: "Marj" is our Irish member, and has all the sunshine, blarney and general ableness of her countrymen. A squeaky door annoyed her, but we understand that there were other "squeaks" (?) which she didn't mind.

name: Baumeister, Emeline
description: "Zip" has just found out she has a voice, although we have suspected it and told her as much. Her laugh is positively contagious—you ought to hear it ripple. "Zip," boom, bah! "Here comes our six-footer."

name: Walton, Ina Blanche
motto: "And her 'yes' once said to you, Shall be 'yes' forever more."
description: One of our Seniors who believes in doing, not talking, and one who always comes out on top in everything she does. You may not know it, but she is by no means a saint when she's in for a good time. Ina is her real self, and the right kind of a friend.

name: Mcartor, Alice Naomi
motto: "Men may come and men may go, But I go on forever."
description: This tall, slender, dark-haired girl is one of Kent State Normal's best. Firmly believes that life is worth living, and never gets "blue." Her quiet way is very charming and she is a general favorite.

name: Whitmore, Elizabeth
description: Here is a happy girl with rosy cheeks and the sweetest of dimples. She has been described as jolly, good-natured and a friend to be proud of. Betty argues a great deal for "Wilhelm" the Kaiser.

name: Keenen, Gladys Wyeth
motto: "Let the world slide! Let the world go! A fig for care and a fig for woe!"
description: Our "Glad" girl, and leading lady in every sense of the word, who believes,

name: Hodges, Mona M.
motto: "Be gone dull care, thou and I shall never agree."
description: Mona is strongly opposed to undue mental or physical exertion of any kind. Her favorite slang expression: "Well, that gets my goat."

name: Hall, Marie Lucile
major: Attended Wooster Summer School, Domestic Science
description: Advocates the study of the Indian "Redman." Does her school work systematically. In her countenance, with its dark, flashing eyes, one may read "industry, thoughtfulness and diligence." She is always hustling, and in affairs at Kent State Normal, is a "booster" and not a "knocker."

name: Conley, Ruth Luella
description: Ruth never quibbles, but gives you her honest opinion in the frankest possible way. When words fail her, she just giggles. "Exams just scare me pink," and yet Ruth never flunks. It is thought that she will marry a minister.

name: Brooks, Mary Marguerite
description: Takes life seriously and very seldom smiles, but when she does it is whole-heartedly. She is very ambitious and goes at things with a vim.

name: Usher, Pearl T.
motto: "A perfect woman, nobly planned, To warn, to comfort, to command."
description: Neat and tidy and quiet, With her dark eyes full of fun, She makes a fine companion For anyone. A Pearl of great price.

name: Colburn, Maude Elsie
motto: "The heart to conceive, the understanding to direct, and the hand to execute."
description: A person you can depend upon, and one you are always glad to see. She is a genuine working officer in the Y. W. C. A. Maude believes in saying what she thinks and we admire her for it.

name: Johnsen, Olga Marie
motto: "Whose heart untouched by love as yet Is wild and fancy free."
description: A girl who would rather roam through the woods and fields and look for pictures in the clouds than study. She insists upon a constitutional three times a day. She is gay, full of fun and song, a fine companion for vacation.

name: Cobbs, Ansalem J.
description: From the town of Beloit Comes a very smart "gink." To relate some exploit He never has to think. He taught village school For over two years; With hickory stick rule He taught the young dears. He's attended Mt. Union and Damascus High, He's surely to be a very smart "guy." This fair youth's name is Anselam Cobbs, Here's hoping he gets the best of jobs.

name: Williams, Laura Jeffries
description: The girl with an elegant air. What has caused Laura's recent interest in dramatics? You will find the "Reason" in the dramatic orchestra. Laura tells us that she believes in "education for present needs," but we don't believe it for if she does she certainly would change her course of study to that connected with household arts.

name: Burrell, Tessa Orilla
motto: "A maid she was, to everyone most dear."
description: One of our girls who has a disposition that is never ruffled by fate or fortune. A bright, sunshiny personage and always well-met. Does not believe in deciding anything in a hurry.

name: Tidd, Thressa Lillian
description: There was a young lady named, So earnest in all that she did, So kind and so true, She's one 'mongst a few, So here's to this lady named.

name: Beckwith, Olive Mildred
description: A bonnie, blooming lass, always looking on the bright side of life, and ever ready to lend a helping hand to those who need it. A born teacher and that profession will lose a valuable member when she leaves for the more congenial task of home-making. One of her favorite pastimes is taking snap-shots.

name: Myers, Margaret Marie
description: Margaret is one of our musicians, and is it any wonder that we all envy her ability along this line, when she is permitted to play for the boys' Glee Club and the men's Orchestra? She is very quiet, even when she speaks, for her voice is so "soft and low." Is a very familiar figure in the library, conning the "Normal" books.

name: Johnson, Zelda E.
motto: "Wise to resolve and patient to perform."
description: Zelda is one of those girls you can always depend on, quiet and business-like but ready for fun when her work is finished.

name: Swartout, Edith
motto: "Modesty makes us fancy there is something very nice beneath it."
description: She is a slight, dainty creature, quiet and very, very shy, and yet when one knows her she is a jolly good girl.

name: Sullivan, Anna L.
high school: Thompson High School, Wooster Summer School
motto: "Who goeth a-borrowing, Goeth a-sorrowing."
description: This seems to be her motto. Always willing to help a friend, insisting upon giving a feast when only bread is asked, but refusing to accept a favor. Her amusements are of a city spirit nature, and also ice boating. She may be a trifle distant, but is well worth an effort to know her.

name: Sullivan, Ellen F.
high school: Thompson High School, Wooster Summer School
motto: "Get leave to work In this world,—'tis the best you get at all."
description: Miss Ellen and her twin (for she is one-half of our only twin group to graduate this year), are here on leave of absence from the Akron schools. She claims to have a pure Irish temper, but we have our doubts. How could one so pleasant and smiling possess a temper? Another thing, we know that there is absolute harmony in all things between the Sullivan twins.

name: Sullivan, Nora E.
high school: Thompson High School, Wooster Summer School
motto: "Oh, pensive scholar, what is fame?"
description: The other "half."

name: McClure, Lulu Florence
description: Blessed with good common sense and a happy disposition. Always willing to do what she can to help a good cause. A faithful worker on committees, etc. Her interests are varied, and she is well liked by her fellow students.

name: Gifford, Edna M.
description: She never seems to worry, She's never in a flurry, But takes things just about as they come. On exams she never frowns, But all her trouble drowns, In that look that speaks content, through and through.

name: Ormes, Mabel Francis
motto: "Zealous, yet modest; innocent, though free; Patient of toil; serene amidst alarms; Inflexible in faith; invincible in arms."
description: Studious and quiet, "Mab" is always ready to do for others in her kind, gentle way and makes friends wherever she goes.

name: Paulmann, Louise M.
motto: "There is but one philosophy and its name is fortitude; to bear is to conquer our fate."
description: "Polly" had had a great deal of experience both in teaching and along another line entirely outside of school work. Because she cannot endure whistling when she studies, one might think that she is not fond of music, but she will assure you that this is not true. She prides herself upon her economy.

name: Killian, Charlotte
description: With all her fun, Charlotte is a student. We must look twice to see whether it is Charlotte or Ruth.

name: Oyster, Lura Lucy
description: What more could we say about her than to say that she is a true friend? Always willing to help those who need it. Although Lura has been with us two years she does not seem to be able to break away from certain associations in her home town. She is very anxious to be "Coy." We wonder why.

name: Morrall, Mary Ann
description: A lassie with a will of her own. A little (?) choice about some things—men, for instance. It would certainly be a treat to see Mary's Ideal. They say that true love never runs smoothly, but Mary and "Mac" don't believe this.

name: Jelley, Sarah Langman
motto: "Valuables," they say, "come done up in small packages."
major: Oberlin College and Conservatory, Domestic Science
description: Here's quality not quantity! Who ever saw her when she was not busy? Tackles everything with a will and even History of Education holds no terrors for her.

name: Jelley, Eleanor Bennett
major: Oberlin Conservatory, Otterbein University, Normal Training Class, Lorain; Graduate K. S. N., Class 1914; Domestic Science
description: It isn't everybody who can graduate twice in two years as Miss Eleanor has, but everybody isn't like her. We at first wondered why she was studying chemistry, but soon found out that she was very much interested in pharmacy. She says she is always going to teach and never have "a better half," but we scarcely believe her. She is very neat and trim in appearance and is a real student.

name: Kent, Mary Amanda
description: A dainty maiden, Fair and sweet, Others with her Dare not compete. Although Mary does not pursue the study of agriculture, she seems to be able to express some good ideas on that subject. She has told us that this is not a good year for "Pumpkins." We can't understand this, for how could "Pumpkins" help but thrive in the sunshine of her smile?

name: Ellis, Rose
description: We see, as we walk down one of the halls, A sweet little maid from Chagrin Falls. Her hair is dark as a raven's wing, Her dainty voice makes the old place ring. This little Rose Ellis, so the Normal girls say, Studies and studies 'till break of day. She works so hard on "History of Ed." She forgets all about time for going to bed.

name: Witsaman, Elva
motto: "As frank as rain On cherry blossoms."
description: Elva meets every task with a resolution and cheerfulness which we all admire. She is the kind who believes grumbling does not pay. A girl who has won a place in the hearts of many at K. S. N.

name: Kingzette, Genevieve Lucille
motto: "Independence now, Independence forever."
description: Doesn't waste any time and can do more in a minute than some people can in an hour. It doesn't take long to find out her opinions on a subject. When you hear that laugh and some of that slang you know Genevieve is around.

name: McFarland, Curney V.
motto: "Health and cheerfulness mutually beget each other."
description: We have no adjectives to describe Curney. And why? Just because she is our Curney, I guess. Just herself—yes—a queen. Everyone in general, and a certain young engineer in particular, will testify that she is a girl among girls.

name: Williamson, Lura May
motto: "The reason firm, the temperate will, Endurance, foresight, strength, and skill."
description: The youngest member of our class in years but not in thoughts. Conscientious and full of fun.

name: Carpenter, H. K.
major: Attended Case School of Applied Science, Cleveland, Ohio
description: His face is stern and sharp and long, the color of blackboard chalk. "The typical school-master," we hear you say. But this Carpenter has been a faithful worker for K. S. N. He is business from the word "go" and is some pusher. We congratulate Prof. Layton on having such an expert assistant. "Carpic" is a frequent visitor at the druggist's. "Is he taking medicine?" we ask. "Yes—love medicine." Congratulations, old "Carp."

name: Cramer, Evelyn Marie
description: Unselfish and innocent describes our "Eve." More than one "Normal" lad has felt the witchery of her eyes. She is the star (?) of the Manual Training class and is envied by her girl classmates for her speed and skill in this art. How does she do it?

name: Sibson, Lois Elizabeth
description: "Sibby" is a worker, always doing her level best. Everything she does, from leading a Y. W. C. A. meeting to getting up Larkin Soap Orders, she does with efficiency and grace.

name: Ortscheidt, Rose
motto: "A rose as sweet as it's bonnie."
description: This little lassie gives you the impression that she is glad she is living, not only to enjoy life herself, but to brighten the lives of others with her smiling countenance and bright witticisms. Truly this "Rose" is dainty and sweet and one that someone some day will be glad to keep.

name: Crittenden, Arleen Harriette
motto: "She had a head to contrive, a tongue to persuade and a hand to execute any mischief."
description: When first you meet her she impresses you with her dignity and queenliness, but after a few words her eyes twinkle and hearty laughter bubbles out, and you find she isn't as stern as she looks.

name: Crittenden, Cecile Etelka
description: Studious to a fault, and frequently encamped behind a forbidding sign "Keep out unless you have business," and yet you know that she is a sociable creature and has the kindest of hearts.

name: Hiestand, Nellie Muriel
motto: "Be good and you'll be happy; But you'll miss lots of fun."
description: Our is one of those persons who are always ready to lend a helping hand to anyone who needs it. Her says she has a lovely disposition, and she ought to know. Is always at work.

name: Tuttle, Harriet Ruth
description: The studious, pious girl of the class with a of her own. Enjoys making baskets, especially ones. is her favorite expression. Is often seen going to or from cooking class. We are pretty sure that her domestic science will be very helpful by and by.

name: Fuetterer, Bertha Carolyn
motto: "Her shortest answer is doing."
description: She goes at everything with a vim from taking naps to coaching a basket ball team. Athletics is not her only hobby—cooking is another. No one can ever forget those rosy cheeks—(and it is all real, too).

name: Murray, Edith Louise
motto: "Here's to a lass whom we know is 'true blue.'"
description: Here we have an ambitious Physical Training girl, fair in face and charming in manner. Her pleasant smile will surely be missed next year. Wherever her future work lies, we prophesy nothing but success for her.

name: Budd, Millo Orpah
motto:
"For she was jes' the quiet kind
Whose nature never varies,
Like streams that keep a summer wind
Snow-hid in January."
description: Under all her primness and reserve there is many a spark of fun.

name: Stratton, Marian Elizabeth
motto:
"The firste vertue, sone, if thou wilt lerne,
Is to restreynye and kepen wel thy tounge."
description: Did you ever hear Marian when her tongue was unrestrained? No? Neither did we, nor shall we ever. She has learned a great truth and is dumb except when there is need for a word. A plain lass, she looks at her work from a teaching standpoint only even though it is Domestic Science.

name: Wheeler, Minnie E.
description: In thinking she is independent, In studies like the sun resplendent, Sometimes has a cold reserve, Which makes the fellows lose their nerve.

name: Jenkins, Ruth La Verne
description: Our friend "Jerineky," who is a shark at music and omniscient with regard to "movie" actresses and photoplays. Is it any wonder we can't fathom why she is taking "Household Arts"? But then the Fates must know how they intend to apply her knowledge.

name: Kroeger, Elsie A.
description: Elsie is a girl worth finding out, sweet-tempered, sympathetic and generous. She has ever a cheerful word as she passes you by.

name: Welsh, Leo Arthur
high school: Chardon High School, Auburn High School
description: He early in his course received the title "Doctor" because of his hidden power of hypnotism. Agriculture and girls seem to be his specialty. When meeting you on the street he always has a smile and a word of encouragement. Besides being something of a "shark" he has always been an all-around good fellow and his success is assured.

name: Burgin, Helen Yetta
motto: "The only reward of virtue is virtue; The only way to have a friend is to be one."
description: Helen has eyes that look into the very soul, and if there is any good to be found there, she will find it.

name: George, Blanche A.
description: Pretty, graceful, winsome, and a little bit bashful -what more could one desire? Tasty in dress and a modern "Anne of Green Gables." Her hair "of sunset hue" and her rosy complexion give tone and color, which Miss Williams advocates in reading class.

name: Dayton, Minnie A.
description: A faithful worker in any position. She believes in saying what she thinks. Books are her joy and constant companions.

name: Wortman, Minnie Belle
description: A maid of intellect is she, As bright and witty as can be. Through great difficulties and sorrows Always a path can see; But one flaw, and that a slang phrase, Whenever excited she exclaims "Hully gee!"

name: Oswald, Marcella Marie
description: There is a girl named "Marcel," Who at Kent Normal doth dwell. Her chances for a "Trip" Would make your sides split If a certain story I should tell. Mary is the most famous talker in Walden Hall. We can't understand why the Dramatic Club failed to discover this. She would make Daniel Webster ashamed of himself.

name: Hamilton, Gladys
description: Powerfully interesting girlie. She knows "Carp's" brother.

name: Cormany, Cora
description: A diligent talker.

name: Daily, Grace
description: If silence is golden, what is "Grace"?

name: Lee, Lila
description: Great thoughts, like great deeds, need no trumpet.

name: Graves, Floyd
motto: "Let me have music and girls and I will seek no more delight."

name: Mosher, Frances
motto: "Talent is like a river, the deeper it is, the less noise it makes."

name: Kennedy, Helen
motto: "She, for what special fitness I know not."

name: Marsh, Kathryn
motto: "Her sweet looks the cottage will adorn, Sweet as the primrose peeps beneath the thorn."

name: Shuart, Marie
motto: "For should I burn or break my brains, Pray who would pay me for my pains?"

name: Hallock, Glenna
motto: "He who invented work should have finished it."

name: Ingell, Baisee
motto: "If I could only dance tonight, I would get my lessons right."

name: Merills, Evelyn
description: There was a girl at Normal School and she was wondrous wise.

name: Meub, Caroline
motto: "Would that my horse had the speed of her tongue."

name: Menough, Jack
motto: "Oh Majesty! 'Tis great to be a king. But greater is it yet to be a man."

name: Bissell, Nita
motto: "She is most easily fooled who imagines that she cannot be."

name: Julian, Mrs.
motto: "Tell me not in idle jingle, marriage is an empty dream, for a girl is dead that's single; and things are not what they seem."

name: Mitchell, David
motto: "He is of stature passing tall, but sparsely lean and spare withal."

name: Tidd, James
motto: "Some folks know more than they can tell; while others refuse to tell much of which they know."

name: Robertson, Olive
motto: "We know her by the great faithfulness with which she does her work."

name: Dayton, Bernice
description: Coquetry is a net laid by the vanity of women to ensnare that of man.

name: Scott, Mildred
motto: "Age cannot wither her, nor custom stale her infinite variety."

name: High, Florence
motto: "Her name implies all that she is."

name: Tressler, Vida
motto: "If she ever knew an evil thought, she spoke no evil word."

name: Bell, Agnes
motto: "She is not a Dumb Bell."

name: Ritchie, Gertrude
motto: "I know it is no sin, for me to sit and grin."

name: Wolfe, Edith
motto: "Like a delicate blossom, she cannot stand the withering blast of constant contradiction and argument."

name: Wilson, Helen
description: A girl is known by the company she keeps, and incidentally this girl is keeping company.

name: Robinette, Gladys
description: Gladys is our Silent Sphinx.

name: Manfrass, Elsie
description: Diligence is the mother of good fortune.

name: Randall, Myrtle
motto: "Oh! Solitude and Silence bid me learn a little of your greatness."

name: Shaw, Lucile
motto: "Diligence is the mother of good fortune."

name: Arnold, Gertrude
motto: "There may be some smarter than she, but we doubt it."

name: Buck, Bernice
motto: "We girls are such poor creatures, slaves of circumstance and fate."

name: Fosnight, Sarah
description: Her superlatively simple sayings, uttered by so sensible a girl, always create sensations.

name: Fowler, Ruth
description: Ruth is a bright girl and has won much favor by her winning smile and pleasing personality.

name: Lower, Agnes
description: One of her attractive features is her smile.

name: Rex, Louise
description: She represents many of the properties of an animated jumping jack, and has a habit of exploding in the midst of an ominous silence.

name: Ingersoll, Mrs.
description: She is a shining light in all her classes.

name: Statts, Leona
description: She has a propensity for music and frequently arouses us from our habitual lethargy by some classical reproduction.

name: Thompson, Helen
description: She and Margaret are as thick as molasses and stick together like two burrs.

name: Tuttle, Helen
